calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar May-20-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Ernest Der for Debra Dolch (Administrator).

The Court stated that it could not make any finding as to what happened in the real estate transaction. Mr. Der explained that they did a preliminary distribution at the request of the Beneficiaries in order to do the buyout and that he believes the $60,000.00 reserve is adequate. The Court granted the accounting petition (subject to review of the order).

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar May-01-2013 Continued To May-15-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION

Off Calendar Feb-11-2013 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Filing Of Status Report Or A Petition For Final Distribution
Off Calendar Jan-14-2013 Continued To Feb-11-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Ernest Der for Debra Dolch.

Mr. Der requested a four week continuance stating that they are still waiting for waivers of accounting and that they have received three out the four. Mr. Der also stated that the buyout is completed and that the funds have been distributed. Status on filing of a petition for final distribution or status report was continued to February 11, 2013 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Paul Alvarado. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy Benton.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Filing Of Status Report Or A Petition For Final Distribution
Off Calendar Dec-17-2012 Continued To Jan-14-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Off Calendar Sep-24-2012 Granted Per Written Order

Report Of Status Of Administration And Petition For Order Continuing Administration
Off Calendar Sep-10-2012 Continued To Sep-24-12 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition To Approve Settlement
Off Calendar Dec-21-2010 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Timothy Chambers, Esq. for Beneficiaries of the estate Ken Lee and Sein Lui. Ernest Der, Esq. for Petitioner/Administrator Debra Dolch.

The Court addressed the issue on waiver of the former and current wife and status on the family issue. Mr. Der reported that he had circulated e-mail to all counsel, his office does not have any address for Mary and Andrew Lee was the one who tried to make contact with the separated spouse and to get her information about this petition for the settlement, her response was that she wants nothing to do with this family. Mr. Chamber stated that Mary Lee has been noticed and no objection for the court approval. Mr. Der confirmed with the court that he did received an e-mail from Mr. Brunwasser about a week a go that said there's really nothing he can do that way he is not present in court today, and requested the court to off calendar the petition to confirm ownership of estate/real property since there is a settlement in place.
Case settled, the petition to confirm ownership of estate real property matter is ordered off calendar at Mr. Der's request. The Court approved the settlement agreement pending the court signing of an order with settlement agreement attached.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: S. Ravinantapricha. Bailiff: Deputy Santiago.

Petition To Confirm Ownership Of Estate Real Property
Off Calendar Dec-07-2010 Off Calendar

Petition To Approve Settlement
Off Calendar Nov-23-2010 Continued To Dec-07-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Ernest Der for Debra Dolch.

Mr. Der stated that there was an issue with notice to the ex-wives of two of the Heirs, that he secured a waiver of notice and consent from Anita Lee but that Mary Lee expressed displeasure of hearing about Gloria's estate, that Mary has not responded, and that he did not have an address for her or a way of contacting her. In addition, Mr. Der stated that it was his impression that the transfer of the property may have been done for refinancing purposes and requested a continuance. The matter was continued to December 7, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. Anasse.
